enonce.texte:
Match the extended family members with their correct definitions.

1. Grandfather
2. Grandmother
3. Uncle
4. Aunt
5. Cousin
6. Nephew
7. Niece
Definitions:
A. The sister of your mother or father
B. The brother of your mother or father
C. The father of your mother or father
D. The son of your uncle or aunt
E. The mother of your mother or father
F. The daughter of your brother or sister
G. The son of your brother or sister

questions.texte: Match each word with the correct definition by writing the correct letter (A-G) in your notebook.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct possessive pronoun: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs

questions.texte: This book belongs to me. It is ______.
This pen belongs to you. It is ______.
This bag belongs to my brother. It is ______.
This dress belongs to my sister. It is ______.
This house belongs to our family. It is ______.
These toys belong to the children. They are ______.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with a, an, or the.

questions.texte: My mother is ______ teacher.
My uncle is ______ engineer.
______ book on the table is mine.
She has ______ aunt in Douala.
______ man at the door is my grandfather.
I have ______ cousin in Yaoundé.

enonce.texte:
Read the text and answer the questions.

My Extended Family

My name is Marie. I am from Yaoundé. I have a big extended family. My grandfather is called Papa Jean. He is 70 years old. My grandmother is Mama Rose. She is 65 years old. They live in the village.

My father has two brothers. Their names are Uncle Paul and Uncle Joseph. My mother has one sister. Her name is Aunt Marie. I also have two cousins. Their names are Jean and Patrick. Jean is my uncle's son. Patrick is my aunt's son.

I have a nephew. His name is Jean-Pierre. He is my sister's son. I also have a niece. Her name is Pauline. She is my brother's daughter. My family is very big!

questions.texte: What are the names of Marie's grandparents?
questions.reponse: Her grandparents are Papa Jean and Mama Rose.

questions.texte: Identify Marie's two uncles.
questions.reponse: Marie's two uncles are Uncle Paul and Uncle Joseph.

questions.texte: Who is Patrick? Explain the relationship.
questions.reponse: Patrick is Marie's cousin. He is her aunt's son.

questions.texte: What is the relationship between Jean-Pierre and Marie?
questions.reponse: Jean-Pierre is Marie's nephew. He is her sister's son.

questions.texte: Analyse the family relationships in the text and create a simple family tree showing Marie, her parents, grandparents, uncles, aunts, and cousins.
questions.reponse:
Family Tree:

Grandparents: Papa Jean + Mama Rose

↓

Marie's Father + Marie's Mother

↓

Marie's father's brothers: Uncle Paul, Uncle Joseph

Marie's mother's sister: Aunt Marie

↓

Marie's cousins: Jean (Uncle Paul's son), Patrick (Aunt Marie's son)

Marie's sister → Jean-Pierre (nephew)

Marie's brother → Pauline (niece)

enonce.texte: Read the description and identify the family member.

questions.texte: My mother's father is my ______.
My brother's daughter is my ______.
My father's sister is my ______.
My mother's brother is my ______.
My uncle's daughter is my ______.
My sister's son is my ______.

enonce.texte: Read the sentences and choose the correct possessive pronoun.

questions.texte: This is my book. The book is ______.
That is his car. The car is ______.
These are our family photos. They are ______.
That is her dress. The dress is ______.
This is your pen. The pen is ______.
These are their toys. The toys are ______.

enonce.texte:
Read the description of the family photo and answer the questions.

Our Family Photo

This is a photo of my extended family. In the photo, you can see my grandparents. My grandfather is sitting on the chair. My grandmother is standing next to him. My parents are behind them. My father is wearing a blue shirt. My mother is wearing a beautiful dress.

My uncles and aunts are also in the photo. Uncle Jean is on the left. His wife, Aunt Sophie, is next to him. Uncle Pierre is on the right. His wife, Aunt Claire, is next to him. My cousins are sitting in front. Jean-Paul is my cousin. He is Uncle Jean's son. Marie-Claire is my cousin. She is Uncle Pierre's daughter.

The photo also shows my nephew and niece. Julien is my nephew. He is my sister's son. Chantal is my niece. She is my brother's daughter. I love my big family!
images: A family photo showing grandparents sitting in chairs, parents standing behind them, uncles and aunts on each side, and children sitting in front.

questions.texte: Who is sitting on the chair in the photo?
questions.reponse: The grandfather is sitting on the chair.

questions.texte: Describe what the father is wearing.
questions.reponse: The father is wearing a blue shirt.

questions.texte: Who is Jean-Paul? Explain his relationship to the narrator.
questions.reponse: Jean-Paul is the narrator's cousin. He is Uncle Jean's son.

questions.texte: Who is Marie-Claire? Explain her relationship to the narrator.
questions.reponse: Marie-Claire is the narrator's cousin. She is Uncle Pierre's daughter.

questions.texte: What is the narrator's feeling about their family?
questions.reponse: The narrator loves their big family.

enonce.texte:
Complete the paragraph with a, an, or the.

My extended family is very big. My grandfather is (1) ______ very kind man. He is (2) ______ oldest person in our family. My grandmother is (3) ______ excellent cook. I have (4) ______ uncle who is (5) ______ engineer. He works in (6) ______ hospital in Douala. My aunt is (7) ______ teacher. She is (8) ______ best teacher in our school. (9) ______ family photo is on the wall. It shows all my cousins. (10) ______ photo is very beautiful.

questions.texte: Complete the paragraph with the correct articles.
questions.reponse: 1. a, 2. the, 3. an, 4. an, 5. an, 6. the, 7. a, 8. the, 9. The, 10. The

enonce.texte:
Write a short paragraph (about 80-100 words) describing your extended family.

Include:

  • Your grandparents (names and ages if possible)
  • Your uncles and aunts
  • Your cousins
  • Your nephews and nieces (if any)
  • Use possessive pronouns and articles correctly

questions.texte: Write a paragraph describing your extended family.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

My name is Fils and I come from a big family. My grandfather is Joseph and my grandmother is Rose. They are very kind. My father has two brothers. Uncle Jean is a doctor and Uncle Paul is a teacher. My mother has one sister, Aunt Marie. She is a nurse. I have many cousins. Jean-Paul is my favourite cousin. He is Uncle Jean's son. Marie is my cousin too. She is Aunt Marie's daughter. I also have a nephew called Jean-Pierre. He is my sister's son. My family is very important to me.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: A Letter to a Friend

enonce.texte:
Read the letter and answer the questions.

Dear John,

I hope you are well. I want to tell you about my family. My family is very big. My grandfather is 80 years old and my grandmother is 75 years old. They are from the village of Nkongsamba.

I have three uncles and two aunts. Uncle Pierre is my favourite uncle. He is my mother's brother. He is a farmer. Aunt Florence is my father's sister. She is a shopkeeper. I have four cousins. My cousin Marie is my best friend. She is the daughter of Aunt Florence.

I also have a nephew called Jacques. He is my brother's son. He is 5 years old. Jacques is very funny! My niece is called Amina. She is my sister's daughter. She is 3 years old.

Please come and visit my family. They will love to meet you!

Your friend,
Marie

questions.texte: Who is Marie writing to?
questions.reponse: Marie is writing to her friend John.

questions.texte: Where are Marie's grandparents from?
questions.reponse: Marie's grandparents are from the village of Nkongsamba.

questions.texte: What is the relationship between Uncle Pierre and Marie?
questions.reponse: Uncle Pierre is Marie's uncle. He is Marie's mother's brother.

questions.texte: Who is Jacques and what is his relationship to Marie?
questions.reponse: Jacques is Marie's nephew. He is her brother's son.

questions.texte: Based on the letter, identify and describe four different relationships in Marie's extended family.
questions.reponse:
1. Grandparents: Marie's grandfather (80) and grandmother (75) from Nkongsamba.

2. Uncles: Marie has three uncles. Uncle Pierre is her favourite. He is her mother's brother.

3. Aunts: Marie has two aunts. Aunt Florence is her favourite aunt. She is her father's sister.

4. Cousins: Marie has four cousins. Marie is her favourite cousin. She is Aunt Florence's daughter.

5. Nephew and Niece: Jacques is Marie's nephew (brother's son). Amina is Marie's niece (sister's daughter).

enonce.texte:
Read the family tree information and answer the questions.

Family Information:

  • Jean and Marie are married. They have two children: Pierre and Sophie.
  • Pierre is married to Anne. They have two children: Paul and Claire.
  • Sophie is married to Luc. They have one child: Jean-Pierre.
  • Paul is married to Catherine. They have one child: Marie-Claire.

questions.texte: What is the relationship between Jean and Pierre?
What is the relationship between Sophie and Jean-Pierre?
What is the relationship between Paul and Claire?
questions.reponse: Paul and Claire are siblings (brother and sister). They are Pierre and Anne's children.

questions.texte: What is the relationship between Jean-Pierre and Marie-Claire?
questions.reponse: Jean-Pierre and Marie-Claire are cousins. Jean-Pierre is the son of Sophie (Pierre's sister) and Marie-Claire is the daughter of Paul (Pierre's son).

questions.texte: Draw a family tree based on this information.
questions.reponse:
Family Tree:

Jean + Marie
   |
   |-- Pierre + Anne
   |      |
   |      |-- Paul + Catherine
   |      |       |
   |      |       |-- Marie-Claire
   |      |
   |      |-- Claire
   |
   |-- Sophie + Luc
          |
          |-- Jean-Pierre

intitule: Writing: A Letter About Your Family

enonce.texte:
Write a letter (about 100-120 words) to your pen pal describing your extended family.

Include:

  • Information about your grandparents
  • Information about your uncles and aunts
  • Information about your cousins
  • Information about your nephews and nieces (if any)
  • Use possessive pronouns (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs) and articles (a, an, the) correctly

questions.texte: Write a letter to your pen pal about your extended family.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Your Address
Yaoundé, Cameroon
15th June 2025

Dear Peter,

How are you? I hope you are well. I want to tell you about my extended family. My family is very big and we love each other.

My grandfather is called Jean and my grandmother is called Rose. They are 80 and 75 years old. They are very kind. My father has a brother, Uncle Paul, and a sister, Aunt Marie. My mother has two brothers, Uncle Jacques and Uncle Pierre.

I have many cousins. Paul is my cousin. He is Uncle Paul's son. Marie is my cousin too. She is Aunt Marie's daughter. They are my best friends. I have a nephew called Jean-Pierre. He is my sister's son. He is 5 years old.

Please write back and tell me about your family.

Your friend,
Fils
